$schema: OIO Apex brand tokens — extracted from oio-apex-brand-guide.html and HANDOFF.md. Single source of truth for all video components; nothing downstream should re-derive a brand value.

rust.role: vintage/patina only — not a mood pick

flag.role: confirmation/pricing signal only — not a mood pick

condensedBlack.role: display/punch only, never below H2, no italic ever
condensedBlack.note: Possibly orphaned per HANDOFF.md — hero punch moved to wide Helvetica Bold/Black. Still used for corner-label numerals/leaderboard digits per this project's design direction.

signPainter.role: vintage script, hero sizes only (120-160px), flat color only — no gradient/outline/drop-shadow

cornerLabel.rule: ALWAYS all-caps. left = fact (year/make/model or event category), right = name/sub-fact. Box always sits on the outer frame edge. Box always contrasts with its own background: white box on a dark shot, black box on a light shot. Unboxed side matches the frame color, not the box.
cornerLabel.allCapsNote: Locked by Ian 2026-07-23 after a mixed-case label shipped on a reel. Enforced in the renderers, not left to callers, so a hand-written prop, a config file and a scripted render cannot disagree: LowerThird.tsx (which EventDate and VenueTag ride on), CornerLabel.tsx, SocialLink.tsx, and social-card's card-draw.mjs. Applied before text measurement everywhere, since the box is sized from the measured string and caps are wider.

caption.note: Burned-in caption card (forced subtitles). Added 2026-07-23 so the Remotion component and the Node captioning pipeline read one set of numbers instead of each hardcoding their own — the pipeline has to measure candidate line widths before it renders anything, so it needs the sizes and the box padding independently of React.
caption.rule: All-caps, one line, hard cut in and out (no fade). A caption SET picks ONE size from fontSizes — the largest at which every line in the set fits the frame — and uses it for every card. Never mix two sizes in the same run of captions: size that changes card to card reads as a glitch, since the viewer has no way to know it carries no meaning.
caption.fontSizesNote: h2 / h1 / heroSm on the type scale. Default came down from 72 per Ian 2026-07-23: at the top of the range a line only carried ~30 characters before running out of a 1620px frame, which chops speech into more cards than it needs. 58 buys roughly 40% more characters per line and still reads on a phone.
caption.paddingNote: Ratios of the font size (was a flat 28px/64px at 72px), so the box keeps its proportions across the whole size range instead of looking gutter-heavy as the type comes down.
caption.safeAreaNote: Where a caption may sit without landing under a platform's own chrome, in px against a 1080x1920 vertical master. `bottom` is the height of the bottom UI stack (caption text, username, audio/music row, progress bar); `side` is per-side horizontal breathing room; `rightRail` is the width of the vertical action-button column (like, comment, share) measured from the right edge. APPROXIMATE — taken from app screenshots at 1080x1920, not from published specs, and these layouts shift between app versions. Re-measure against a real post before treating any of it as exact. `vertical` is the union (worst case of every platform), so one render is safe everywhere; render per-platform only when reclaiming that space actually matters.
caption.maxCharsNote: Hard cap on characters per caption line. null = derive it from the frame width (landscape lands around 42 on a 1620px frame). Vertical is capped at 12 per Ian 2026-07-23: short-form captions want to be punchy and turn over fast, and it is that cap — not any layout trick — that solves the platform-UI problem. A 12-character centred box is narrow enough to clear the action rail by geometry (a centred box clears a 250px rail whenever it stays under frameWidth - 2*rail = 580px on a 1080 frame), so vertical captions can stay centred instead of being anchored off to one side to dodge the buttons.
caption.alignNote: Everything centres. Vertical briefly anchored left to dodge the action rail — the rail sits ABOVE the bottom UI stack, so clearing the bottom stack is not enough, and a centred long line measured x=994 against a rail starting at x=830. Capping vertical lines at 12 characters removed the need: the box is now narrow enough to clear the rail centred, which reads better and keeps the caption where the eye already is.

shape.note: Locked 2026-07-18 (HANDOFF.md §Square corners), formalizing a convention that was already implicit — info pills and corner labels never had rounding — but had never been written down. Added to this file 2026-07-23 to close a gap where theme.ts already exported tokens.shape and Foundations/Shape already read it, so the story threw and tsc failed on every build.
shape.rule: Square corners: every box, label, card, pill, and button uses hard right-angle corners — no border-radius. The circle brand system (badges, rank circles, connector marks) is the one exception, and it is always fully round, never partially rounded.

verticalVideoLower._comment: the leaderboard's own share of a 1080-wide vertical canvas that also carries a 16:9 landscape clip above it: 1080 * 9/16 = 608px for that clip, 1920 - 608 = 1312px left for the board (~68%).

social.note: Sizes are % of export width (cqw) so badge/label stay the same real size across every export aspect — see brand guide section 06 and SocialFrame.tsx. Badge/label doubled from the original 96px/30px-at-1080 figures per Ian, 2026-07-18 — original numbers read too small against real feed posts. cornerLabelFontSize brought back down to 3.2cqw the same day: at 5.56cqw the doubled size clipped long real content ("1982 HONDA PRELUDE") into an ellipsis inside the 36cqw maxPartWidth box — 3.2cqw is the largest size that fits that real caption content with no ellipsis, measured via a Playwright binary search against the actual CornerLabel/SocialFrame render, not eyeballed. badgeDiameter halved again same day (17.8cqw -> 8.9cqw) per Ian, reviewing a real headless-rendered card — the doubled size read too big once seen at real export resolution instead of in Storybook's preview scale. This value is the single source of truth. A separate standalone Social Post Maker artifact (published at claude.ai, used for mobile posting — NOT retired, Ian actively uses it) has its own independently-hacked local copy of this badge math (13.35cqw as of 2026-07-18) that was never updated to match this file's later halving to 8.9cqw, and has no embedded-font fix or square/wide aspect support either — it is a known-drifted third copy of this brand math, not a mirror of it. Don't assume the artifact matches tokens.json without checking; resyncing it was out of scope for the 2026-07-18 session that wrote this note.
social.gridCropSafety: Tried raising badgeOffset/cornerLabelOffset to 6cqw/9.5cqw on 2026-07-18 to survive Instagram's profile-grid center-crop (see git history for the math). Ian reverted same day — read as too far inset from the corner at the original offset's intended scale. Back to the shared 2.22cqw both elements had before. Profile-grid crop vulnerability (portrait/wide lose the badge+label entirely when IG squares them for the grid view) is therefore a known, accepted tradeoff, not unresolved — don't re-attempt the same fix without asking first.
